current.summary: Resolved MOAT spec issues #6-7 with OWASP cross-reference validation; issue #8 (revocation) ready for next session

activeWork.description: MOAT spec outline security framework alignment and OWASP standards mapping
activeWork.progress: Issues #6 (scan_status field design) and #7 (risk_tier classification) resolved with full decision text. Researched and mapped 6 OWASP standards (AST, ASI, LLM Top 10:2025, API Security, CI/CD, Web App Top 10:2025) to spec coverage. Updated moat-revised-outline.md with resolved decisions and OWASP alignment tables. Identified 3 new partial gaps (CICD-SEC-8, API2/7/10). Committed and pushed. Issue #8 (revocation) ready to begin.

recentDecisions: Issue #6: scan_status uses {not_scanned, clean, findings}; resolves S5 coarseness, aligns with AST08/AST04/LLM03/CICD-SEC-9
Issue #7: risk_tier uses {not_analyzed, indeterminate, L0-L3}; enables capability classification, aligns with AST09/ASI04/LLM04
Established two-list OWASP distinction: Agentic Skills Top 10 (AST, GitHub) vs Agentic Applications Top 10 (ASI, genai.owasp.org)
nextSteps: Begin issue #8: revocation framework (token lifecycle, endpoints, federation trust)
Apply same OWASP cross-check methodology to revocation design (expect ASI06, AST05, AST10 guidance)

context.notes: Working directory: /home/hhewett/.local/src/moat. File path: docs/moat-revised-outline.md.
